rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allyl 2-arylbenzimidazoles with CF3SO2Na to access CF3-bearing benzimidazo-fused tetrahydroisoquinolines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the photoexcited eosin Y catalyst (and/or oxidizes the cyclized radical to a cation); CF3SO2Na is oxidized via SET to release CF3 radical after extrusion of SO2

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical after CF3 addition to allyl alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical from intramolecular arene attack
Wheland-type cation (after anodic oxidation of cyclized radical)

electron_flow: Photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to generate a CF3 radical (after SO2 loss), which adds to the terminal allyl alkene of 1a; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the pendant aryl ring, and the cyclohexadienyl radical is further oxidized (anodically and/or via eosin Y+) to a cation, then deprotonated to give 3a. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; CF3-DPE adduct 18 isolated in 36%, confirming CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: on/off control: no PC/no light
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction profile shows greatly diminished/no formation of 3a without photocatalyst and light

probe_reagents.name: on/off control: no electricity
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction profile shows greatly diminished/no formation of 3a without electrolysis, confirming synergy of photo- and electrocatalysis

notes: Trapping experiments collectively support a radical pathway initiated by CF3 radical generated via SET oxidation of CF3SO2Na; isolation of CF3-DPE adduct 18 (36%) provides direct evidence for free CF3 radical. SET inhibitor (pDNB) abolishes reactivity. On/off control reactions (no PC/light or no electricity) demonstrate both photochemical and electrochemical components are required, consistent with a photoelectrochemical radical-polar crossover cyclization.
